response.text:
# REFLECTION

r2c1 is in critical survival mode with 11.5% SoC (0.23/2 kWh) and a 1.0 kW load, providing only ~4.6 hours until complete depletion while islanded with no generation observed. The memory log reveals a network-wide cascading failure: r1c1, r2c0, and r2c2 are all rejecting outbound transfers due to their own critical deficits (10–38% SoC ranges, 22-minute autonomy windows). r2c0 has made repeated normal-priority requests, but r2c1 cannot afford to share without breaching its own survival threshold. r3c1 has been marked distrusted based on prior rejection reasoning. All peers are uniformly enforcing self-preservation policies, indicating cluster-wide scarcity and mutual inability to help. The only viable path forward is strict inbound-only posture, escalation to aggregator-level coordination, and hope for grid reconnection or external generation. Outbound sharing at this SoC would be suicidal; even minimal transfers risk crossing the point of no return.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.80
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers:
  - r3c1
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Network-wide cascading failure; all peers in critical deficit (10–38% SoC). r2c1 at 11.5% SoC with ~4.6 hr autonomy. Zero generation observed. Outbound sharing prohibited—survival threshold non-negotiable. Inbound requests from r2c0 (normal priority) deferred pending grid reconnection or aggregator intervention. No trust circles established; all P2P sharing suspended."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
